title: Unit 11: AVL Tree
description: A self-balancing binary search tree where the height difference of any node's subtrees is at most 1.

topics.title: 11.1 AVL Insertion & rebalancing
topics.description: Perform standard BST insertion followed by balance factor updates and LL, RR, LR, or RL rotations if unbalancing occurs.
topics.cheatSheet:
// Left Rotation
Node y = x.right; x.right = y.left; y.left = x; updateHeight(x); updateHeight(y); return y;

ispo.desc: Accepts subtree root node 'y' to rotate right.
Defines AVL Node including height variable, which tracks balancing metrics in memory.
Allocates local variables x and T2 to track rotation pivots.
Alters node pointer connections to shift subtree levels and recalculates heights of rotated nodes.
Returns the reference of the new subtree root node.

quiz.q: What is the balance factor of a node in an AVL tree?

topics.title: 11.2 Deletion in AVL Tree
topics.description: Perform standard BST deletion, then traverse up the tree to update heights and perform balance rotations if needed.
topics.cheatSheet: Perform BST delete. Update height. If balance factor is not in [-1, 1], perform rotations.

ispo.desc: Takes AVL root node pointer and key value to delete.
Allocates temporary node reference pointer 'temp'.
Stores balance factor value in balance variable.
Performs standard BST deletion to remove key and updates tree structure.
Updates height and checks if balance factor is in [-1, 1], performing rotations if out of bounds.
Returns null if tree becomes empty.
Returns updated balanced node.

quiz.q: What is the worst-case time complexity of Deletion in an AVL tree?

topics.title: 11.3 Count Greater Nodes in AVL Tree
topics.description: Calculate how many nodes in an AVL tree have values strictly greater than a given parameter value x.

complexity.time: O(n) worst case, but can be optimized if node sizes are stored

topics.cheatSheet: Inorder traversal. If node.val > x, increment count, recurse both sides.

ispo.desc: Accepts root node and target value x.
Creates temporary integer current to store match indicator.
Compares node.val with x. If node.val > x, recurses both left and right; else, only checks right subtree (since left values are smaller).
Returns final matched count.

quiz.q: If node.val is less than or equal to x, why do we skip searching the left subtree?
o: In a BST, all elements in the left subtree are smaller than or equal to the parent, so they cannot be greater than x
It is done to save memory

topics.title: 11.4 Minimum Nodes for height H in AVL Tree
topics.description: Find the minimum number of nodes required to build an AVL tree of height H. Solved using recurrence: N(H) = N(H-1) + N(H-2) + 1 with N(0)=1, N(1)=2.

ispo.desc: Takes height H.
Declares result variable to hold calculated recursive sum.
Checks base levels. Computes count by adding nodes needed for (H-1) and (H-2) heights plus 1 node for root.
Returns calculated minimum nodes.

quiz.q: What is the minimum number of nodes in an AVL tree of height 3?
